item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche was pressed during his testimony at a Senate Appropriations Subcommittee hearing over who could be compensated from the Justice Department’s anti-weaponization fund. Sen. Chris Coons, D-Del., questioned Blanche whether President Donald Trump’s campaign donors or those who assaulted law enforcement during the January 6 riots could receive compensation.

media:content.media:title: 'Anybody can apply': Blanche pressed over who can get compensation from DOJ fund
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche was pressed during his testimony at a Senate Appropriations Subcommittee hearing over who could be compensated from the Justice Department’s anti-weaponization fund. Sen. Chris Coons, D-Del., questioned Blanche whether President Donald Trump’s campaign donors or those who assaulted law enforcement during the January 6 riots could receive compensation.

item.title: 'Anybody can apply': Blanche pressed over who can get compensation from DOJ fund

item.description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo
media:content.media:description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

item.title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo

item.description: Two teenaged gunmen opened fire at the Islamic Center of San Diego, killing three adults. Soon after, a vehicle was found with the bodies of both the alleged shooters inside. San Diego police tell NBC News that the mother of the 17-year-old suspect called police hours earlier, sharing she believed her son was suicidal, was wearing camouflage, left behind a note and that both her car and several firearms were missing. NBC’s Morgan Chesky reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Teen Gunmen Open Fire in San Diego Mosque, Killing 3
media:content.media:description: Two teenaged gunmen opened fire at the Islamic Center of San Diego, killing three adults. Soon after, a vehicle was found with the bodies of both the alleged shooters inside. San Diego police tell NBC News that the mother of the 17-year-old suspect called police hours earlier, sharing she believed her son was suicidal, was wearing camouflage, left behind a note and that both her car and several firearms were missing. NBC’s Morgan Chesky reports for TODAY.

item.title: Teen Gunmen Open Fire in San Diego Mosque, Killing 3

item.description: Voters are heading to the polls for primaries across six states, including Kentucky, where the campaign of Rep. Thomas Massie, a repeated Trump critic, is being seen as a crucial test of President Donald Trump’s power on the Republican party. Meanwhile, the Justice Department set up what it calls an “anti-weaponization fund,” using nearly $1.8 billion of taxpayer money to pay Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports and

media:content.media:title: Trump Targets Kentucky Rep. Thomas Massie in Tuesday Primary
media:content.media:description: Voters are heading to the polls for primaries across six states, including Kentucky, where the campaign of Rep. Thomas Massie, a repeated Trump critic, is being seen as a crucial test of President Donald Trump’s power on the Republican party. Meanwhile, the Justice Department set up what it calls an “anti-weaponization fund,” using nearly $1.8 billion of taxpayer money to pay Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports and Meet the Press moderator Kristen Welker joins TODAY with key races to watch.

item.title: Trump Targets Kentucky Rep. Thomas Massie in Tuesday Primary

item.dc:creator: Allie Canal
item.description: Home Depot, Lowe’s, Walmart and Target report earnings this week, offering a clear picture of how consumers are faring amid inflation and high energy costs.

media:content.media:text: Home Depot Ahead Of Earnings Figures
media:content.media:description: The Home Depot will report quarterly earnings Tuesday.

item.title: Are consumers cracking under the weight of high prices? We’re about to find out

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: Vladimir Putin landed in China on Tuesday to a warm welcome from his “dear friend” President Xi Jinping, but he arrived with the heavy burden of a war he increasingly seems unable to win.

media:content.media:text: Russian President Vladimir Putin with Chinese President Xi Jinping at the Zhongnanhai leadership compound in Beijing on Sept. 2, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin with Chinese President Xi Jinping at the Zhongnanhai leadership compound in Beijing on Sept. 2, 2025.

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Trump Meets With China's President Xi At Zhongnanhai
media:content.media:description: During Trump's visit, Xi described the relationship between the U.S. and China as the world's most important.

media:content.media:text: RUSSIA-UKRAINE-CONFLICT
media:content.media:description: Ukraine’s increasingly effective drone arsenal is taking a mounting toll on Russia.

item.title: Goodbye Trump, hello Putin: China’s Xi welcomes Russian leader facing pressure at home

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is calling off an attack on Iran planned for Tuesday because regional leaders had urged him to allow negotiations to continue and a “very acceptable” deal for the U.S. was at hand.

media:content.media:text: People walk on a street near a mural featuring an image of the late Supreme Leader of Iran, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran
media:content.media:description: People walk on a street near a mural featuring an image of the late Supreme Leader of Iran, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran, Iran, May 18, 2026.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-IRAN-US-ISRAEL-WAR-PROTEST
media:content.media:description: Women hold Iran's national flags during an anti-U.S. and -Israel protest at a square in Tehran on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: Iran US
media:content.media:description: The Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi's western desert.

media:content.media:text: Iran And US Remain In Stalemate Over Strait Of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: A ship anchored Saturday in the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island, Iran.

item.title: Trump says he called off a planned attack on Iran

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
Joe Kottke
item.description: Tom Kane, who voiced characters in the “Star Wars” franchise and Professor Utonium in Cartoon Network’s “The Powerpuff Girls,” died Monday from complications of a stroke he suffered in 2020

media:content.media:text: US-FILM PREMIERE-STAR WARS THE CLONE WARS
media:content.media:description: Tom Kane at the U.S. premiere of "Star Wars The Clone Wars" in Hollywood in 2008.

item.title: ‘Star Wars’ and ‘Powerpuff Girls’ voice actor Tom Kane dies at 64

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: Progressives are cautiously optimistic that they can capture the nomination for a congressional seat in Philadelphia in a race against the Democratic Party’s center-left establishment Tuesday

media:content.media:text: Chris Rabb leans on a metal gate outside while extending a fist out to someone and smiling
media:content.media:description: Congressional candidate Chris Rabb at a campaign event at Garden of Prayer World Prayer Center in Philadelphia.

media:content.media:text: Sharif Street stands outside on a residential sidewalk and crosses him arms to pose for a portrait
media:content.media:description: Congressional candidate Chris Rabb at a campaign event at Garden of Prayer World Prayer Center in Philadelphia.

media:content.media:text: Ala Stanford stands outside in front of the Dr. Ala Stanford Center for Health Equity building
media:content.media:description: Congressional candidate Chris Rabb at a campaign event at Garden of Prayer World Prayer Center in Philadelphia.

item.title: Progressives seek another big primary win in the nation’s bluest House district

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: Vladimir Putin landed in China on Tuesday to a warm welcome from his “dear friend” President Xi Jinping, but he arrived with the heavy burden of a war he increasingly seems unable to win.

item.title: Goodbye Trump, hello Putin: China’s Xi welcomes Russian leader facing pressure at home

item.dc:creator: Patrick Smith
item.description: More than $1.4 million has been raised for the family of Amin Abdullah, the security guard fatally shot Monday at a San Diego mosque while protecting the lives of more than a dozen children and staff at a school inside

media:content.media:text: Islamic center of San Diego active shooter
media:content.media:description: Multiple people were killed, including an armed security guard, after two shooters entered Islamic Center in San Diego Monday, May 18, 2026, and opened fire.

media:content.media:text: Amin Abdullah.
media:content.media:description: Amin Abdullah.

media:content.media:text: US-RELIGION-CRIME
media:content.media:description: Two women cry as they leave a reunification center following the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego in Southern California, on Monday.

media:content.media:text: Emergency services respond to reported active shooter near San Diego Mosque
media:content.media:description: Emergency services are deployed in an area close to the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

item.title: ‘Heroic’ security guard killed protecting children and staff in San Diego mosque shooting

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: The death toll from an Ebola outbreak in central Africa rose sharply Tuesday, with the head of the World Health Organization expressing concern over the “scale and speed of the epidemic.”At least 131 people are believed to have died and 531 suspected to have been infected, according to the Congolese Health Ministry.

media:content.media:text: A visitor washes his hands at a sink operated by a hospital worker before entering Kyeshero Hospital
media:content.media:description: A visitor washes his hands before entering Kyeshero Hospital at a checkpoint for hand washing and temperature screening for all visitors and patients entering Kyeshero Hospital, as part of Ebola prevention measures, in Goma , Democratic Republic of Congo, on May 18, 2026.

media:content.media:text: DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: A visitor's temperature is checked before entering Kyeshero Hospital in Goma on Monday.

item.title: Ebola deaths rise sharply as outbreak’s ‘scale and speed’ worry WHO chief

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Bridget Bowman
item.description: President Donald Trump’s campaign to suppress dissent in his own party faces its most prominent test yet as he takes aim at Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky, while GOP candidates across the country look to leverage the president’s endorsement in key primaries.

media:content.media:text: DHS Oversight House 3/04/26
media:content.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., during the House Judiciary Committee hearing titled "Oversight of the Department of Homeland Security," in Rayburn building on March 3, 2026.

media:content.media:text: DOnald Trump, left, and Burt Jones speak near a crowd of people inside of a restaurant
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump and Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones together in Rome, Ga., on Feb. 19.

item.title: Trump loyalty tests, major battlegrounds and more: What to watch in Tuesday’s elections

item.dc:creator: Allie Canal
item.description: Home Depot, Lowe’s, Walmart and Target report earnings this week, offering a clear picture of how consumers are faring amid inflation and high energy costs.

item.title: Are consumers cracking under the weight of high prices? We’re about to find out

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Mosheh Gains
item.description: The inspector general will look at the intelligence and targeting Southern Command used for the strikes, as well as other aspects of the operations against drug cartels, according to a May 11 letter from the Pentagon’s Office of Inspector General.

media:content.media:text: The Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia.
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon launched Operation Southern Spear, its name for the campaign against alleged drug-smuggling boats, in 2025.

media:content.media:text: Smoke rises from a boat after a U.S. strike in the Eastern Pacific on April 13, 2026.
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon launched Operation Southern Spear, its name for the campaign against alleged drug-smuggling boats, in 2025.

item.title: Pentagon’s internal watchdog to probe U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins
item.description: The Justice Department said it would drop its criminal fraud charges against billionaire Indian businessman Gautam Adani, a significant reversal in a high-profile case.

media:content.media:text: Gautam Adani, chairman of Adani Group
media:content.media:description: Gautam Adani, chairman of Adani Group, last year.

item.title: U.S. prosecutors drop fraud charges against billionaire Indian businessman

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
item.description: Chan’s competition includes state Sen. Scott Weiner and wealthy former tech executive Saikat Chakrabarti, a co-founder of the progressive group Justice Democrats.

media:content.media:text: Representative Nancy Pelosi
media:content.media:description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., announced in November that she would not seek re-election.

media:content.media:text: San Francisco Congressional District 11 Candidate Forum
media:content.media:description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., announced in November that she would not seek re-election.

item.title: Nancy Pelosi endorses fellow progressive Connie Chan in high-profile race to succeed her

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Brennan Leach
Margaret Faust
Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy defended his 2021 vote to convict President Donald Trump on impeachment charges, saying he would rather be remembered for standing up for his principles even if it ended his career in the Senate.

media:content.media:text: Bill Cassidy gestures with one hand while speaking into a microphone inside a U.S. Capitol building.
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., said he wouldn't disagree with President Donald Trump simply to be at odds with him following his bid for a third Senate term.

item.title: After primary loss, Sen. Bill Cassidy defends his 2021 vote to convict Trump

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Dog sunscreen protects pets from sunburn, skin cancer and sun damage. What kind of sunscreen is best for dogs, according to vets? Can I put human sunscreen on my dog?

media:content.media:text: All dogs benefit from wearing SPF, especially in areas with little hair coverage like their noses, ears, stomachs and groins.
media:content.media:description: All dogs benefit from wearing SPF, especially in areas with little hair coverage like their noses, ears, stomachs and groins.

media:content.media:text: All dogs benefit from wearing SPF, especially in areas with little hair coverage like their noses, ears, stomachs and groins.
media:content.media:description: All dogs benefit from wearing SPF, especially in areas with little hair coverage like their noses, ears, stomachs and groins.

item.title: Do dogs need sunscreen? Vets say yes — here’s why

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: The popular Ninja Slushi frozen drink and dessert maker is on sale for 26 percent off, putting it back at its lowest price ever. It’s is perfect for making frozen treats and more.

media:content.media:text: The Ninja Slushi is available in an original version and a Max model.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Slushi is available in an original version and a Max model.

media:content.media:text: The Ninja Slushi is available in an original version and a Max model.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Slushi is available in an original version and a Max model.

item.title: The Ninja Slushi is back at its lowest price ever just in time for frosé season

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best early Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Leesa, Ninja, Levoit, Supergoop and Apple.

media:content.media:text: Shop early Memorial Day deals early to avoid potential out of stock products and shipping delays.

item.title: Memorial Day Sales 2026: The best 45+ early deals to shop now

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Experts say the best Memorial Day deals are on summer essentials, mattresses, appliances and more. Plus, the two worst things to buy during sales.

media:content.media:text: Retailers want to get rid of warm weather essentials while shoppers are thinking about them, so look out for deep discounts on grills, patio furniture and more.
media:content.media:description: Retailers want to get rid of warm weather essentials while shoppers are thinking about them, so look out for deep discounts on grills, patio furniture and more.

media:content.media:text: Retailers want to get rid of warm weather essentials while shoppers are thinking about them, so look out for deep discounts on grills, patio furniture and more.
media:content.media:description: Retailers want to get rid of warm weather essentials while shoppers are thinking about them, so look out for deep discounts on grills, patio furniture and more.

item.title: How to shop Memorial Day like a pro — what to buy and skip this year
